Strip_Summary: POV morning routine - alarm clock
Stickman driving car
column9: kissing child on forehead at kitchen table. Same mug

Strip_Summary: Stickman at door. Child not looking up. Dark background text: 'And that is the point.'
Stickman across from FACELESS doctor in clinical office. Plastic kidney model on desk.
Doctor says words stickman cannot understand. End-stage renal failure. Fluorescent office.
Doctor explaining kidney failure. Stickman absorbing. Kidneys dying for a while.
Stickman in chair processing. You just didn't know. Once they stop
Stickman sitting in car in hospital parking lot. Engine running. Not going anywhere.
Stickman model kidney on desk. Phone buzzing
column9: not looking. Doctor's office parking lot.

Strip_Summary: Dark background. Text: 'You did not choose this.'
Dark background. Text: 'Your body chose for you.' Stickman silhouette
Stickman at transplant office waiting room. Plastic chairs
Talk show playing at low volume. Everyone looks tired. Hospital waiting room.
Stickman filling forms. Medical history
Stickman pausing at 'relationship to patient' line. Writing 'wife'. Heavier than expected.
FACELESS clerk handing number 847. Stickman folding paper
Stickman looking at wallet with folded number. Don't know why keeping it there.
Stickman in dialysis chair. Machine cleaning blood. Tubes connected to fistula in arm.
Needle doesn't hurt anymore. That bothers stickman. Book never read past first page.
Stickman staring at ceiling tiles. Counting. Forty-three. Unread book on lap.
FACELESS nurse checking tubes. Shoes squeak on floor. Sound stays longer than doctor's words.
Sleeping FACELESS man in next dialysis chair. Know his breathing rhythm but not his name.
Empty chair next Tuesday. Nobody says anything. FACELESS nurse changing sheets
Three months pass. Number moves from 847 to 843. Four spots in three months.
POV of stickman hand holding updated number slip. Hospital waiting room background.
Dark background. Text: 'Someone dies. You move up.'
Stickman checking number every Friday. Habit like checking weather. Routine gesture with phone.
Stickman with swollen ankles on couch. Twelve hours sleep
Everything tastes like metal. Like chewing a coin. Pushing plate away at dinner table.
Child watching stickman push food away. No questions this time. Worse than questions.
Stickman telling daughter 'under the weather'. Said it for five months. Kitchen doorway.
Stickman scratching wrist absent-mindedly. Dry skin from dialysis tape. Medical wristband visible.
Stickman in doorway watching wife. Face she makes when she thinks you cannot see.
Wife at laptop
column9: life insurance pages. Closes tab when stickman walks in. Neither speaks.

Strip_Summary: Two stickmen on couch watching TV. Not looking at each other. Not sadness. Preparation.
Two stickmen in dialysis chairs. One leaning
column9: whispering. Stickman has noticed for weeks.

Strip_Summary: Whisperer mentions a guy who can help. Outside the system. Says it quietly
Like recommending a restaurant. Stickman shakes head. No thanks. But can't forget it.
Stickman thinking about it in shower. And the next night. And the one after that.
Hotel lobby. Stickman and FACELESS broker in dark jacket. Carpet smells like cleaning product.
Broker gives no name. Stickman doesn't ask. Can get kidney. Four to six weeks.
Somewhere overseas. Clean facility. Licensed surgeon. Clinical
Broker describes it like vacation brochure - price
column10: destination. Should never be a brochure.

Strip_Summary: One fifty thousand like refrigerator price tag. Coffee between them goes cold.
Don't need to know details. Don't touch coffee. Just be ready. Hotel lobby table.
Stickman saying no. System works. Wait your turn. Standing
Broker nods. Slides receipt with phone number. Convenience store receipt. Keep it. Just in case.
Receipt into wallet next to waiting list number. Two numbers. Two paths.
Not looking at either on drive home. Three weeks later emergency room. Wife drove at 2 AM.
Daughter still asleep. Don't wake her
column9: would scare her. Meant: would make this real.

Strip_Summary: Creatinine spiked. Doctor says six months. Maybe less. Hospital room
Doctor says it gently. Like that helps. Wife in hallway
IV drip clicking every few seconds. Stickman starts counting. Hospital bed
Lying in bed at night. House quiet. Daughter's birthday in seven months. Doing the math every night.
Six months minus three for decline minus two for complications. Might not make it to spring.
Imagining daughter opening presents without stickman. Thought doesn't finish. Faces wall.
Wife breathing in next room. Not sleeping either. What kind of father dies on waiting list.
Stickman with closed eyes. Text: 'You already know what you're going to do.'
Number in wallet next morning. Three weeks old. Receipt soft from folding too many times.
Stickman in car
column9: driveway. Engine off. Windshield fogging from breath.

Strip_Summary: Daughter's pink backpack on back seat. Keychain she picked out. Looking at it.
Stickman making call in fogged car. Broker answers second ring. 'Knew you'd call back.'
Can you travel? Yes. Have the money? One fifty thousand. Already calculated everything.
Retirement savings. College fund. She'll understand someday. Or won't. Alive to find out.
Saying yes. Date and city incoming. Not asking where kidney comes from.
Dark background. Text: 'You don't ask anything.' Stickman silhouette on phone.
Stickman in half-empty parking lot after call. Streetlight buzzing. Don't remember starting engine.
Stickman arriving in foreign city. Warm heavy air. Taxi driver asking about business.
Told wife medical conference. Told daughter back by Friday. Promised to bring something.
Promised. Looked up souvenir shops. In case she asks where you went. Preparing alibi.
Unmarked clinic entrance. Bleach smell in hallway. FACELESS staff not looking up.
No introductions. Someone takes bag
column9: hands gown. Nobody says name. Different kind of number.

Strip_Summary: POV from operating table. White ceiling
column9: bright lights. Stickman hands at frame edges.

Strip_Summary: Finger clip
column9: monitor beeping. Anesthesiologist counting backward. Sounds from other room.

Strip_Summary: Machine sound
column9: voice from other room. Don't know what's happening there.

Strip_Summary: Sounds from adjacent room. Built survival on that not-knowing. Dread tint fade.
Fingers grip table. Half-second tremble. Then close. Nobody sees. Micro-crack moment.
Dark background. Bold text: 'You chose not to know.'
Stickman waking in recovery bed. Smaller room. Open window
Hand on abdomen. Something inside that was not there yesterday. Quieter than expected.
It belonged to someone. Someone in a room near yours. Recovery bed
Dark background. Text: 'You will never know who.' Stickman silhouette in bed.
FACELESS nurse handing form. Voluntary donation. Family member. Stickman reading once.
Signing false declaration. Pen heavy. Don't remember. Flight home seven hours by window.
Sitting by window. Don't read
column10: don't think. Antiseptic smell on hands.

Strip_Summary: Washing hands in airplane bathroom. Smell won't go away. Reflection looks the same.
Dark background. Bold text: 'That is the strangest part.'
Stickman at home door. Daughter runs to greet. Says you look better. She's right.
Color returning to face. Feeling energy
column10: hunger - terrified knowing what it cost.

Strip_Summary: Wife holding stickman in doorway. Not letting go for long time. Not asking about trip.
Stickman smiling. Eyes don't match mouth. Daughter handing drawing she made.
Drawing of park - you and her. Holding it like glass. Puts on fridge with magnet.
Looking at drawing every morning. Opening pill bottle. One pill. Immunosuppressant.
Body will reject what's inside. Pill stops that. Every day for rest of life.
Daughter picked up pill bottle once. Tried to read label. Took it from her gently.
She didn't ask again. Nobody asks. Text: 'Every pill is a clock that resets.'
Two stickmen at dinner. Friend asks about transplant. Family donor? Cousin. Overseas.
Very generous. Rehearsed so many times sounds true. Fake cousin David or Daniel.
Doesn't matter - cousin doesn't exist. Friend nods
Hand is steady - surprising. Think you understand. You don't. Re-hook moment.
Morning kitchen. Headline: organ trafficking ring dismantled. Twelve arrests.
Reading two lines. Turning page. Hands shaking. Putting down paper
Everything blurs - kitchen
column10: daughter's voice - then comes back. Near-break.

Strip_Summary: Blinks. Nobody noticed. Daughter asks what's in the news. Nothing interesting.
Phone rings while doing dishes. Number recognized. Voice recognized. Offering liver
Like reading from menu. Stickman hangs up. Doesn't delete number. Don't know why.
Phone face down on counter. Back to dishes. Warm water.
That's it. Kitchen evening light. Warm water running.
Morning callback. Same mug
column9: same coffee. Driving to work. Kissing daughter on forehead.

Strip_Summary: Daughter barely looks up. Pill bottle next to coffee maker. Everything looks the same.
Inside you something that will never be yours. Text: 'Never ask who it belonged to.'
Dark background. Text: 'If you've ever wondered what it feels like...' Stickman silhouette.
Dark background. Final text: 'Now you know.' Stickman silhouette with pill bottle.
